open_known_gaps.summary: Client-side memory-exhaustion (PDF-bomb DoS) class — tracked, no server impact (a user can only exhaust their own browser tab). Load boundaries still lack a numPages>1000 / cumulative-byte cap and a processing timeout. Consumers: (1) viewer.js loadDocument() renders one canvas per page (linear; render-race amplifier FIXED+VERIFIED in TASK-316); (2) thumbnails.js renders a SECOND canvas per page into the Pages panel (doubles per-page footprint; mitigated by 120px thumb width, dpr cap 2, sequential render, renderToken guard, but page COUNT still unbounded); (3) merge.js (TASK-319) caps each file at 50MB but not cumulative queued bytes/pages; (4) watermark.js operates only on the already-validated open doc (not a new boundary). Single right fix = developer task: a page-count/byte cap at the viewer.js LOAD boundary protects viewer + thumbnails + every future per-page consumer at once. Documented in docs/security-guide.md.

audit_notes.innerHTML_in_js: only comments or empty-string clears (layer.innerHTML='') — no user/PDF content sink
audit_notes.external_fetch_or_xhr: none (100% client-side, PDFs never leave the browser)
audit_notes.split_js_download_filename: sanitized via replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9._-]/g,'_') then blob URL + a.download, URL revoked next tick — path-traversal safe
audit_notes.annotate_js_labels: aria-label noun sourced from controlled TOOLS enum, not file content; all mark text via textContent/setAttribute
audit_notes.secrets_scan_hit: status/security.json only — matched the literal words 'git_secrets'/'secrets_in_tree' in this file's own keys; false positive, no real secret
